opp.ais_why:
• Per the 11 May 2026 rep note from Luke Dougherty: 'Have provided an update that still working with business on requirements, am trying to get a demo session or an AI session, said she will get back to me this week.' The rep is explicitly offering a dedicated 'AI session' to Brickworks - meaning AI is being positioned as a wedge to re-engage a cool customer rather than the customer pulling for it.
• Per the 13 Apr 2026 rep note: 'They are working through requirements and will be in touch for a demo in approx 4 weeks' and the 2 Oct 2025 record: 'Daniel Jackson - Consultant (Argon Consulting) and Brad Hurst (Procurement & Supply chain Manager)... Interested in Direct and Indirect capabilities of Zycus.' Argon Consulting is driving an Argus-style traditional S2P RFP - the buyer language is requirements/Direct/Indirect, never AI or Merlin or autonomous.
• Medium confidence: products in scope include the full Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA stack so AI is at least technically on the table, NextStep is detailed across 9 dated entries, but Avoma returned zero meetings for this opp so no buyer-side transcript quote exists. Brad Hurst and Daniel Jackson have not been quoted naming any AI capability. Boundary call defaulted DOWN to 5.
